verses.text: The burden of the desert by the sea comes as a 'grievous vision,' filling the prophet with pain and dread. The word of judgment weighs on him. To foresee coming ruin, even of enemies, is a heavy load for the faithful heart.

While men feast and prepare the table, the watchman is set to watch, and he sees a chariot of horsemen approach. Revelry ignores the danger. The wise keep watch while the careless dine.
While men feast and prepare the table, the watchman is set to watch, and he sees a chariot of horsemen approach. Revelry ignores the danger. The wise keep watch while the careless dine.

The watchman cries like a lion, then announces, 'Babylon is fallen, is fallen; and all the graven images... are broken.' The proud city collapses. Her idols, helpless to save her, are smashed in the dust.

To the cry, 'Watchman, what of the night?' he answers, 'The morning cometh, and also the night.' Light and darkness both lie ahead. There is brief relief, then renewed trouble, for those without God.
To the cry, 'Watchman, what of the night?' he answers, 'The morning cometh, and also the night.' Light and darkness both lie ahead. There is brief relief, then renewed trouble, for those without God.

The burden on Arabia tells the caravans to bring water to the thirsty fugitives, for within a year all the glory of Kedar shall fail. Their strength is short-lived. The mightiest tribes wither at the appointed word of God.
